column2: geoCore input file containing exploratory hole information and how they may to geotechnical clusters/windfarm assets
The file represents the authoratative catalogue of all geotechnical cluster locations and exploratory holes installed at an asset project
Shall be saved in the Geolog directory: "G:\01_Departments\GTSC\Geolog\projects\{area_code}\data\input_files"
Keeps track of input_file revision history
Date of creation or modification of the file (yyyymmdd)
Initials of the GA engineer taking responsibility for the document content
An accuracy level (0-5) shall be assigned to the technical documentation relative to the project phases

column3: Design Calculations: Preliminary. Based on regional experience and/or approximate conditions described.
Examples of use: Determining whether a project can be outright rejected.
Design Calculations: Preliminary. Based on estimates of suitable parameters from similar/neighbouring projects.
Examples of use: Determine possible foundation concepts and preliminary budgets in early project development phases.
Design Calculations: Preliminary. Based on estimated stratigraphy combined with site-specific global parameters (GT1).
Design Calculations: Detailed. Based on position specific stratigraphy and soil parameters (GT2)
column4: input parameters still not fully finalised.

column3: Examples of use: Detailed design calculations
Design Calculations: Detailed. Based on detailed position specific stratigraphy and soil parameters
column4: input parameters not necessarily documented or certified.

column3: Examples of use: Detailed design calculations
Design Calculations: Detailed. Based on certified stratigraphy and input parameters.
Examples of use: Final design calculations. Steel order.

column2: Initials of GA lead engineer performing quality control and assurance of the document
A QA framework (1-3) shall be considered in relation to the WTG foundation design process

column3: QA responsible: Preparer (self-check) - All geotechnical engineers (juniors to be assisted by appointed engineer).
For any deliverable outside Ørsted's GEO dept.
QA responsible: Reviewer - Senior specialist appointed by line manager.
For any deliverable outside Ørsted (e.g. to certification organisations
QA responsible: Reviewer - Senior Specialist appointed by line manager.

column2: Initials of senior specialist performing independent QA of input file content
Initials of line management or senior specialist approval of ensuring consistent quality
Restrictions on use of the file
Detail description of changes and motivation behind the changes introduced in this version of the file
table containing geotechnical exploratory hole cluster information
stable project-scoped internal identifier for the cluster location which must remain independent of layout revisions.
site investigation reference (to be consistent with "LOCA_CLST" AGS terms - as defined in Appendix 2 Annex 1 (00637136_F))

column3: Preliminary geotechnical site investigation campaign (usually scoped and executed by others and subsequently acquired by Orsted)
Lite geotechnical site investigation campaign (typically comprising a limited scope, e.g. shallow CPTs and grab samples only)
Reconnaissance geotechnical site investigation campaign (usually comprises a number of geotechnical cluster locations to facilitate ground model development, unitisation etc.)
Detailed design geotechnical site invesitgation campign (comprises exploratory holes installed at every WTG and OSS location)

column2: ground or seabed level relative to the adopted vertical datum (usually negative for offshore sites)
(sea) water level relative to the adopted vertical datum (usually zero for offshore sites)
table containing exploratory hole details -- this file is the campaign's FINAL, QAQC'd snapshot (see geodb/python/docs/pdtable-etl-mapping.md's "Sequential handoff" section)
column3: every hole_status value below must be a TERMINAL status (never SCHEDULED/INPROGRESS -- those live only in the EHS workbook while the survey is still in progress)

column2: exploratory hole name assigned by the contractor (LOCA_ID in .ags files)

column3: Preliminary geotechnical site investigation campaign (usually scoped and executed by others and subsequently acquired by Orsted)
Lite geotechnical site investigation campaign (typically comprising a limited scope, e.g. shallow CPTs and grab samples only)
Reconnaissance geotechnical site investigation campaign (usually comprises a number of geotechnical cluster locations to facilitate ground model development, unitisation etc.)
Detailed design geotechnical site invesitgation campign (comprises exploratory holes installed at every WTG and OSS location)

column2: reason for installing exploratory hole (can be for a physcial wind farm asset like a WTG or OSS location, or a geotechnical cluster at a reconnaissance/cable location)
exploratory hole number (can be alpha-numeric, e.g. A01)
jacket leg label number. Upper-case letters or numbers
contractor_hole_name of the hole this row bumps over (must be blank if bumpover_label is blank, and vice versa
column3: must not name this row's own contractor_hole_name)

column2: final lifecycle status of the hole. MUST be a terminal status (see reference.hole_status.is_terminal) -- SCHEDULED/INPROGRESS values are rejected, since this file is only ever the campaign's completed, QAQC'd snapshot

column3: Achieved target depth (or contractual acceptance criteria) as planned
Terminated short of target depth but accepted by the project as complete
Terminated prematurely (early refusal, equipment failure, tool loss etc.) and not accepted
Attempted or partially attempted, then abandoned by project decision
column4: will not be completed or bumped over

column3: Removed from the schedule before any attempt was made

column2: planned target depth of the hole below seabed/ground level
actual achieved depth of the hole below seabed/ground level
why the hole stopped at final_depth
column3: blank/NULL for SCHEDULED or CANCELLED holes (see reference.termination_reason)
Refusal above target depth (e.g. cone refusal, spudcan/hard stratum)
Tool, rig or vessel equipment failure
Terminated due to weather or operational downtime
Terminated or abandoned by project/client instruction

column2: date operations commenced at the position (yyyymmdd)
date operations concluded at the position (yyyymmdd)
as-installed easting coordinate, as recorded by the final EHS re-issue; blank if not surveyed/known, in which case the load pipeline falls back to the linked asset/cluster's design position
as-installed northing coordinate, as recorded by the final EHS re-issue; blank if not surveyed/known, in which case the load pipeline falls back to the linked asset/cluster's design position
seabed/ground level as observed at time of works at this specific hole position (m RL) -- distinct from cluster_details.ground_level, which is only an approximate level for the cluster as a whole
